theme: election rule that blunts organized strategic blocs
title: The Bloc-Proof Ballot
strategies: plain plurality: elect whoever has the most first-place votes; trivially demolished by a b-voter COMPROMISE bloc that bullet-votes a weaker contender past the leader
Borda count: sums rank points; slightly steadier than plurality on some sweeps but still swung hard by a bloc concentrating first-place votes on its target

unweighted lowest-index Condorcet completion: restrict to the Smith set but break residual cycles by raw candidate index, so a bloc that manufactures a 3-way cycle can reliably steer the outcome to whichever cycle member has the smallest index

Smith-set restriction + minimax (Simpson) margin tie-break + a ballot-content-keyed deterministic tie-break for any residual tie: never elects outside the top cycle, and among cycle members prefers the one with the smallest worst pairwise defeat, denying the bloc a stable, index-predictable target
